mission.title: Install and Configure Opentelemetry Collector (Opentelemetry) on Kubernetes
mission.description: The OpenTelemetry Collector is a vendor-agnostic implementation for receiving, processing, and exporting telemetry data. Installing it allows you to streamline the collection of telemetry data from various sources and send it to multiple backends without managing multiple agents.

steps.title: Add the OpenTelemetry Collector Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official OpenTelemetry Collector Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the OpenTelemetry Collector
steps.description:
Install the OpenTelemetry Collector using Helm, specifying the desired namespace (e.g., 'otel-collector'):
```bash
helm install otel-collector open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector --namespace otel-collector --create-namespace --version v0.146.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenTelemetry Collector pod is running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace otel-collector
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for local access
steps.description:
To access the OpenTelemetry Collector locally, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/otel-collector --namespace otel-collector 4317:4317
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the OpenTelemetry Collector pod running in the 'otel-collector' namespace. You can access it locally via port forwarding, and resource limits should be configured as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install otel-collector open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector --namespace otel-collector --create-namespace --version v0.146.1

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenTelemetry Collector release using Helm to remove the deployment and associated resources:
```bash
helm uninstall otel-collector --namespace otel-collector
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
If any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) were created during the installation, you can delete them with the following command:
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
```
Replace `<crd-name>` with the actual name of the CRDs. Additionally, if there are any persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with the deployment, remove them:
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace otel-collector
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
If the namespace was created specifically for the OpenTelemetry Collector and is no longer needed, delete it:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace otel-collector
```
Verify that the namespace has been removed:
```bash
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current configuration and state of the OpenTelemetry Collector:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace otel-collector -o yaml > otel-collector-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the OpenTelemetry Collector to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade otel-collector open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector --namespace otel-collector --version v0.147.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check that the OpenTelemetry Collector pod is running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace otel-collector
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenTelemetry Collector pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace otel-collector
```
Replace `<pod-name>` with the name of the pod. Look for configuration errors or resource issues.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the OpenTelemetry Collector service, ensure that the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace otel-collector
```
If the service is not running, check the deployment and pod status for errors.

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high memory usage in the OpenTelemetry Collector, check the resource limits and requests:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment otel-collector --namespace otel-collector
```
Consider adjusting the resource limits in the deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Configuration changes not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If configuration changes are not reflected in the OpenTelemetry Collector, ensure you have updated the correct deployment:
```bash
kubectl get deployment otel-collector --namespace otel-collector -o yaml
```
Verify that the configuration matches your intended changes.

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy the OpenTelemetry Collector.
